reasoning:
Based on the Wikipedia page "FIDE rankings" on November 1, 2025, Lei Tingjie's standard chess Elo rating was 2569. FIDE publishes its official rating lists on the 1st of each month, which Wikipedia subsequently mirrors. Therefore, her rating on November 9, 2025, will be 2569. To resolve as YES, her rating needs to increase by at least 1% (25.69 points), meaning it must reach 2595.

1. **2025-11-16:** Standard FIDE ratings are not updated mid-month. There will be no new rating list released between November 9 and November 16, so her rating will remain statically at 2569. The theoretical probability of this condition resolving YES is close to 0%; we use the minimum allowed value of 0.05.
2. **2025-12-09 & 2026-02-07:** Achieving a 26-point jump at the ~2570 level is exceptionally difficult and would require a massive winning streak in classical events. Recent news indicates Lei Tingjie withdrew from the 2024-25 FIDE Women's Grand Prix for personal reasons earlier in the year, pointing to restricted classical activity rather than heavy tournament schedules. Even if she participates in classical tournaments, gaining +26 points in 1 to 3 months is extremely rare for top-10 world players. 
Thus, the likelihood remains extremely slim, though uncertainty marginally increases as the time horizon extends to February 2026.
